quoteFirst: Bailey is at the foot of a mountain by the same name, and we pulled in there
quoteLast: After stopping only a few minutes, we left the river and started up over Bailey’s Mountain, going, as it seemed on paper, across lots to Denver, but in reality across mountains.
title: The Cruise of a Schooner
author: Harris, Albert W. (Albert Wadsworth), 1867-1958

quoteLast: we began to return, and, after rowing hard for about an hour and a half, were approaching our lonely camp when we heard guns being fired rapidly.
title: Through the Dark Continent, Vol. 2 (of 2)
author: Stanley, Henry M. (Henry Morton), 1841-1904

quoteFirst: "On the 4th of August," says one witness, "at Herve" (a village not far from the frontier), "I saw at
quoteLast: , near the station, five Uhlans [German cavalry]; these were the first German troops I had seen. They were followed by a German officer and some soldiers in a motor car. The men in the car called out to a couple of young fellows who were standing about 30 yards away. The young men, being afraid, ran off, and then the Germans fired and killed one of them named D----."
title:
The Truth About German Atrocities
Founded on the Report of the Committee on Alleged German Outrages
author: Anonymous

quoteFirst: The murder was committed on Palm-Monday, being the fourteenth of April,
quoteTime: about two of the clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: , at which time the said Barwick having drilled his wife along 'till he came to a certain close, within sight of Cawood-Castle, where he found the conveniency of a pond, he threw her by force into the water, and when she was drowned, and drawn forth again by himself upon the bank of the pond, had the cruelty to behold the motion of the infant, yet warm in her womb.
title: Miscellanies Upon Various Subjects
author: Aubrey, John, 1626-1697

quoteFirst: It had been despatched from Vevey
title: The Passenger from Calais
author: Griffiths, Arthur, 1838-1908

quoteTime: about two o'clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: I started in order, by some means or other, to see Fidel and question her.
title: The Mantle, and Other Stories
author: Gogol, Nikolai Vasilevich, 1809-1852; Mérimée, Prosper, 1803-1870 [Contributor]; Field, Claud, 1863-1941 [Translator]

quoteFirst: On the 26th of the same month,
quoteLast: , Mrs. A. was sitting in a chair by the window in the same room with her husband.
title: Letters on Natural Magic; Addressed to Sir Walter Scott, Bart.
author: Brewster, David, 1781-1868

quoteFirst: It was bitterly cold on deck, and
quoteLast: we had had a shower of hail.
title: The Incomparable 29th and the "River Clyde"
author: Davidson, George, 1863-1947

quoteTime: about two o'clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: , the bells of all the churches were rung, and the mitred abbots, and all others of the clergy, went out in procession to meet him dressed in their sacred robes, bearing many relics, who, with chaunting, conducted the king to the cathedral of our Lady.
title: The chronicles of Enguerrand de Monstrelet, Vol. 05 [of 13]
author: Monstrelet, Enguerrand de, 1390?-1453; Johnes, Thomas, 1748-1816 [Translator]
